# Booktest-Scala — AI-Friendly Documentation

> Booktest-Scala is a Scala port of [booktest](https://github.com/lumoa-oss/booktest).
> Review-driven snapshot testing for data science, ML, and any system whose outputs
> aren't strictly right or wrong but require expert review.

## When to Use

- ML model evaluation with tolerance metrics ("accuracy 0.92 ± 0.05 = OK")
- LLM/AI output testing (non-deterministic; needs review, not pass/fail)
- Data pipelines where "close enough" matters
- Tests that produce rich output (tables, images, traces) better reviewed as markdown
- Tests that share an expensive live service (DB, HTTP server, process)

## Installation

```scala
libraryDependencies += "io.github.arauhala" %% "booktest-scala" % "0.4.1" % Test
```

Cross-compiled for Scala 2.12, 2.13, and 3.3.

## Minimal Test

```scala
import booktest.*

class MyTests extends TestSuite {
  def testHello(t: TestCaseRun): Unit = {
    t.h1("Hello")
    t.tln("Hello, World!")  // tested against snapshot
    t.iln("debug info")     // shown but not tested
  }
}
```

Run: `sbt 'Test/runMain booktest.BooktestMain my.MyTests'`. First run writes
`books/my/MyTests/testHello.md` (the snapshot). Subsequent runs diff against it.

## Test API

`TestSuite` (extend this for your tests):

| Method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `def testFoo(t: TestCaseRun): T` | Method-style test (auto-discovered if name starts with `test`) |
| `test(name)(body)` | Method-ref style test, returns `TestRef[T]` |
| `test(name, dep1, dep2)(body)` | With type-safe dependencies (up to 3) |
| `liveResource(name, deps...)(build)` | Shared `AutoCloseable` across consumers (concurrent readers) |
| `liveResourceSerialized(name, deps...)(build)` | Shared, but consumers run one at a time (no reset) |
| `exclusiveResource(name, deps...)(build)` | Per-consumer instance |
| `liveResourceWithReset(name, deps...)(build)(reset)` | Shared, serialized, reset between consumers |
| `capacity(name, total)` | Numeric budget (RAM, CPU shares) |
| `setup(t)` / `teardown(t)` | Per-test hooks (override) |
| `beforeAll()` / `afterAll()` | Per-suite hooks (override) |
| `resourceLocks: List[String]` | Override to serialize tests within suite (parallel mode) |
| `mark(testName, markers*)` | Tag a test (slow, flaky, gpu, etc.) |
| `resources: ResourceManager` | Access to port pool, lock pool, capacities |

`TestCaseRun` (the `t:` parameter; what you call inside a test):

**Output (tested against snapshot)**:
- `t(text)` / `tln(text)` — tested content
- `h1`/`h2`/`h3`/`h4`/`h5(title)` — headers (anchor-seeking)
- `key(label, value)` — labeled value as test line
- `tmetric(label, value, tolerance, direction)` — metric with absolute tolerance, direction = `Some("min")` or `Some("max")` for regression detection
- `tmetricPct(label, value, tolerancePct, direction)` — percentage tolerance
- `tLongLn(value, unit, max, tolerance)` / `tDoubleLn(...)` — value with unit/limit/tolerance
- `ttable(headers, rows)` / `ttable(map)` / `ttable(caseClassSeq)` — markdown tables
- `timage(file, caption)` — image (hashed, stored in assets dir, referenced in snapshot)
- `tUsPerOpLn(opCount, label)(block)` — time block, output µs/op as test line

**Output (info — written to the same `.md` snapshot but not test-fail-on-diff)**:
- `i(text)` / `iln(text)` — info content; mismatches surface as cyan `.` info-diffs (don't fail)
- `imetric(label, value)` — info metric (no tolerance check)
- `itable(...)` / `iimage(file, caption)` — info versions
- `iMsLn(block)` — execute block, log elapsed ms as info, return result
- `iUsPerOpLn(opCount, label)(block)` — info µs/op
- Token alignment between `t` and `i` is per-token, so a length-changing
  info value (e.g. `"7ms"` vs `"0ms"`) does NOT cascade into a diff on
  neighboring `t` content.

**Failure**:
- `f(text)` / `fln(text)` — fail content (always marks test failed)
- `fail()` / `fail(message)` — mark test failed without throwing
- `assertln(condition)` / `assertln(condition, message)` — assert; `iln("ok")` on success, `fln(message)` on failure (NO exception)
- `diff()` — mark current line as diff

**Caching & dependencies**:
- `snapshot[T](name, args*)(compute)` — cache expensive computation, auto-invalidate on arg hash change
- Test return value is auto-cached to `<test>.bin` for dep injection
- `hasSnapshot(name, args*)`, `invalidateSnapshot(name)`, `invalidateAllSnapshots()`
- Build-system semantics: filtered runs (`-t pat`) re-run a transitive dep ONLY if it matches the filter, its `.bin` is missing, or `-r`/`--refresh-deps` is set; otherwise the cached value loads from `.bin` (Python booktest parity)

**Async**:
- `await(future, timeout)` — await with default infinite timeout
- `async { ec => ... }` — run with test's `ExecutionContext`
- `executionContext: ExecutionContext` — cached thread pool

**File management**:
- `file(name)` — File in test assets dir (`books/<suite>/<test>/<name>`)
- `tmpFile(name)` / `tmpDir(name)` / `tmpPath(name)` — under `books/.out/<suite>/<test>/`; cleared on re-run; supports `"/"` for nested paths
- `clearTmpDir()` — explicit clear

**Review workflow** (AI-assisted batch review):
- `startReview()` — begin a review section (header written)
- `reviewln(label, value, passed=None)` — add review item; `passed=None` means needs human review
- `reviewln(label, condition: Boolean)` — shorthand
- `endReview(): Boolean` — true if all items passed/accepted

**Resource access**:
- `acquirePort()` / `releasePort(port)` / `withPort(block)` — port pool

**Snapshot navigation (advanced)**:
- `anchor(prefix)` / `anchorln(line)` — seek snapshot to prefix/line, then write as tested content
- `seek(predicate)` / `seekLine(line)` / `seekPrefix(prefix)` / `jump(lineNo)` — manual cursor control
- `peekDouble` / `peekLong` / `peekToken` / `skipToken` — inspect next snapshot token without consuming

## Live Resources (v0.4.0+)

Share an expensive `AutoCloseable` across many tests. Built once on first
consumer, closed when last consumer finishes; pool/capacity allocations
held for the resource's lifetime.

```scala
class StringServer(port: Int, payload: String) extends AutoCloseable { /* ... */ }

class ServerTests extends TestSuite {
  val state = test("createState") { (t: TestCaseRun) => "hello"; }

  val server: ResourceRef[StringServer] =
    liveResource("server", state, resources.ports) {
      (s: String, port: Int) => new StringServer(port, s)
    }

  test("clientGet", server) { (t: TestCaseRun, http: StringServer) =>
    t.tln(s"GET -> ${http.get("/echo")}")
  }
}
```

Sharing modes: `liveResource` (shared, concurrent readers; default) | `liveResourceSerialized` (shared, one consumer at a time, no reset) | `liveResourceWithReset(...)(...)(reset)` (shared, serialized, reset between consumers) | `exclusiveResource` (per-consumer instance).
Dep types in the dep list: `TestRef[T]` | `ResourceRef[T]` | `ResourcePool[T]` | `ResourceCapacity[N].reserve(amount)`.

**Lifecycle is runner-managed.** Pre-pass refcount-reservation → first
acquire builds (resolves deps, acquires pool/capacity slots) → consumers
borrow under share-mode rules → last release closes (and returns
pool/capacity allocations) → end-of-run `shutdownAll` force-closes any
stragglers. Build runs once on the first consumer's thread under a
per-resource build lock; close runs inline on the last releaser's thread.

**Consumer contract: do not call `.close()` on an injected handle.**
The `T <: AutoCloseable` bound exists for the runner, not for tests.
A consumer that closes its handle (often via Scala's `using { ... }`)
tears the instance down for every later consumer and corrupts their
snapshots silently. If a test genuinely needs to own its instance,
use `exclusiveResource(...)` instead.

`PortPool` (used by `resources.ports`): rotates issued ports rather than
sticking to the lowest free one, and applies a kernel-settle cooldown
between release and re-issue (default 250ms). Override via
`BOOKTEST_PORT_COOLDOWN_MS` env var. Range via `BOOKTEST_PORT_BASE` /
`BOOKTEST_PORT_MAX` (default 10000–60000).

Capacity: `val ram = capacity("ram", 4096.0)`; override via `BOOKTEST_CAPACITY_RAM=8192` or `--capacity ram=8192`.

## CLI

```bash
sbt 'Test/runMain booktest.BooktestMain [flags] [test classes / groups]'
```

| Flag | Effect |
|---|---|
| `-v, --verbose` | Verbose output (lifecycle events, test content) |
| `-i, --interactive` | Interactive accept/reject of changed snapshots |
| `-l, --list` | List discovered tests |
| `-L, --logs` | Show captured stdout/stderr |
| `-w, --review` | Re-show diffs from previous run without executing |
| `-pN` | Parallel execution with N threads (e.g. `-p4`) |
| `-c, --continue` | Skip tests that passed last run |
| `-r, --refresh-deps` | Force re-run of transitive deps (default: load cached deps from `.bin`) |
| `-t PATTERN` | Filter tests by substring (transitive deps still included) |
| `-S, --recapture` | Force regenerate ALL snapshots |
| `-s, --update` | Auto-accept snapshot changes (DIFF and FAIL) |
| `-a, --accept` | Auto-accept DIFF tests only (not FAIL) |
| `--batch-review` | Sequential interactive review of all failures at end |
| `--tree` | Hierarchical tree display (with `-l`) |
| `--inline` | Show diffs inline during execution (default: at end) |
| `--diff-style STYLE` | `unified` (default), `side-by-side`, `inline`, `minimal` |
| `--output-dir DIR` / `--snapshot-dir DIR` | Override default `books/` |
| `--root PREFIX` | Override `root` from `booktest.ini` |
| `--garbage` | List orphan files in `books/` |
| `--clean` | Delete orphan files and tmp dirs |
| `--invalidate-live-on-fail` | Force-close shared live resources on consumer failure |
| `--capacity NAME=VALUE` | Override a `capacity(NAME, _)` total |
| `-h, --help` | Show help |

## Configuration (`booktest.ini`)

Searched upward from current directory.

```ini
root = booktest             # Package prefix to strip from test paths
default = examples, test    # Default group(s) when no args
all = test, examples        # Named group: test classes are looked up under it
exclude = FailingTest, MultiFail   # Suite name substrings to skip in default runs
books-path = books          # Snapshot directory
```

## Environment Variables

- `BOOKTEST_PORT_BASE` — port pool base (default 10000)
- `BOOKTEST_PORT_MAX` — port pool max (default 60000)
- `BOOKTEST_CAPACITY_<NAME>` — override `capacity(name, _)` total (uppercase)

## File Layout

```
books/
├── .out/                      # gitignored — runtime artifacts
│   └── <suite>/
│       ├── <test>.bin         # cached return value (for deps)
│       ├── <test>.md          # raw captured output (compared against snapshot)
│       ├── <test>.log         # captured stdout/stderr
│       ├── <test>.txt         # per-line diff report (?/./! markers, side-by-side) + status — match Python booktest. CI artifact viewers should land here for a flagged DIFF.
│       └── <test>/            # tmpDir for the test
├── <suite>/
│   ├── <test>.md              # COMMITTED snapshot (the spec)
│   └── <test>/                # COMMITTED assets (images etc.)
└── cases.ndjson               # last-run results (continue mode)
```

## Workflow

1. Write a test producing markdown via `t.tln(...)` etc.
2. Run — first time, snapshot is missing → DIFF.
3. Inspect output. Use `-i` to accept, or `-s` to bulk-accept.
4. Snapshot files are committed to git as the spec.
5. Future runs diff against snapshot. Real changes show up as DIFF.
6. Use `-w` to re-review diffs without re-executing.

## Documentation

- `README.md` — overview + quick start
- `USAGE.md` — full API reference and examples
- `CLAUDE.md` — codebase guide for contributors and coding agents
- `CHANGELOG.md` — release history
- `.ai/plan/live-resources.md` + `live-resources-example.md` — design docs for v0.4.0

## Original Python Booktest

Concept and design: https://github.com/lumoa-oss/booktest. This Scala port
follows the Python API and snapshot format closely; differences are
documented in `CLAUDE.md` and `CHANGELOG.md`.
